settingUp: We're setting up your app with AI magic.

bothCommandsRequired: Both commands are required when customizing.
autoUpgradeAnnotator: Auto-upgrade app to enable Dyad annotator features

aiRulesTooltip: AI_RULES.md tells Dyad which tech stack to use when editing the app
appImportedWithRules: App imported successfully. Dyad will automatically generate an AI_RULES.md now.

forceCloseDescription: The app was not closed properly the last time it was running. This could indicate a crash or unexpected termination.
noDiagnosticData: No diagnostic data available for this crash.
communityCodeWarning: This code was created by a Dyad community member, not our core team.
communityCodeRisk: Community code can be very helpful, but since it's built independently, it may have bugs, security risks, or could cause issues with your system. We can't provide official support if problems occur.
communityCodeReview: We recommend reviewing the code on GitHub first. Only proceed if you're comfortable with these risks.
deleteItemConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete "{{itemName}}"? This action cannot be undone.

help.helpOptions: If you need help or want to report an issue, here are some options:
help.helpBotDescription: Opens an in-app help chat assistant that searches through Dyad's docs.
help.openDocsDescription: Get help with common questions and issues.
help.reportBugDescription: We'll auto-fill your report with system info and logs. You can review it for any sensitive info before submitting.
help.uploadChatDescription: Share chat logs and code for troubleshooting. Data is used only to resolve your issue and auto-deleted after a limited time.
help.failedToLoadChatSession: Failed to load chat session. Please try again or report manually.
help.askQuestion: Ask a question about using Dyad.
help.conversationLogged: This conversation may be logged and used to improve the product. Please do not put any sensitive information in here.
help.mustOpenIssue: You must open a GitHub issue for us to investigate. Without a linked issue, your report will not be reviewed.
help.reviewSubmission: Please review the information that will be submitted. Your chat messages, system information, and a snapshot of your codebase will be included.

screenshot.betterResponses: You'll get better and faster responses if you do this!
screenshot.mightNotHelp: We'll still try to respond but might not be able to help as much.
screenshot.capturedToClipboard: Screenshot captured to clipboard! Please paste in GitHub issue.

security_panel.editRulesDescription: This allows you to add additional context about your project specifically for security reviews. This content is saved to the SECURITY_RULES.md file. This can help catch additional issues or avoid flagging issues that are not relevant for your app.
security_panel.securityRulesFullPlaceholder:
# SECURITY_RULES.md

Describe relevant security context, accepted risks, non-issues, and environment details.

testsPanel.isolatedRunHint: During database-isolated runs, other app operations may wait until the run finishes.
testsPanel.askAiToFixTests: Ask AI to fix tests in {{file}}
testsPanel.openChatToFix: Open a chat to ask the AI to fix this test.
testsPanel.sentToChatFix: Sent to chat — asking the AI to fix the test…
testsPanel.openChatToGenerate: Open a chat to generate a test.
testsPanel.sentToChatGenerate: Sent to chat — generating a test…
testsPanel.selectApp: Select an app to view tests.
testsPanel.title: Tests
testsPanel.parallelTitle: Parallel: a file's tests run concurrently (faster, shared dev server)
testsPanel.serialTitle: Serial: a file's tests run one at a time
testsPanel.headedTitle: Headed: tests open a visible browser window
testsPanel.headlessTitle: Headless: tests run without a visible window
testsPanel.isolatedTestDataTitle: Tests ran against a temporary copy of your database — your real data was not touched.
testsPanel.isolatedTestUserTitle: Tests ran as a temporary, isolated test user under Row-Level Security — your real data was not touched.
testsPanel.neonRestartNotice: Neon test runs restart the preview to switch to a temporary database, then restart it again afterward.
testsPanel.startApp: Start the app to run tests.
testsPanel.loadFailedDescription: The test list couldn't be read for this app.
testsPanel.noTestsDescription: Generate your first test, or ask the AI in chat to write one for a specific feature. Generated tests show up here as a starting point you can review and re-run.
testsPanel.generateJourney: Generate a test for a critical user journey
testsPanel.enableDescription: Let Dyad write and run end-to-end tests that drive your app like a real user. Tests are a starting point you can review, edit, and re-run.
testsPanel.neonDataWarning: Tests run against a temporary copy of your Neon database, so your real data isn't touched.
testsPanel.rlsWarning: Tests run as an isolated test user under Row-Level Security, but RLS may not cover every table. We strongly recommend enabling data backups before running tests, in case they do something unintended.
testsPanel.notIsolatedWarning: Dyad can't isolate this database in the current setup. These tests can create, update, or delete current data, so we strongly recommend enabling data backups before running them.
testsPanel.customBackendWarning: These tests can create, update, or delete real data, and Dyad can't isolate a custom or non-database backend. We strongly recommend enabling data backups before running tests, in case they do something unintended.

database.title: Database
database.pickDatabase: Pick the database your deployed app should connect to.
database.productionActiveMessage: Your app is on the production branch, so the production database will be used for deployment. No migration is needed.

production.title: Separate production database
production.description: Keeps live data separate from development. Safest for real users, but database changes require a migration step.

development.title: Use development database
development.description: Uses one database for development and production. Faster setup, but dev changes affect live data.

envVars.title: Environment variables
envVars.description: Add these to your hosting provider's environment variables for the deployed app.

database.syncToVercelHelp: Push database env vars to Vercel and add your deployment domain to Neon's auth redirect allowlist.
database.syncSuccess: Synced database configuration to Vercel, but a redeploy is required for your live app to use it.
database.selectBranchError: Failed to save your database branch selection.
database.clearBranchError: Failed to clear your database branch selection.

migration.title: Database Migration
migration.description: Copy the schema from your active development branch to your production branch.
migration.descriptionWithBranches: Copy the schema in {{projectName}} from {{sourceBranchName}} to {{targetBranchName}}.
migration.alreadyInSync: Your development and production databases are already in sync.
migration.switchBranchHint: Switch to a non-production branch in the Neon panel before migrating.
migration.confirmDescription: This will modify your production database schema. Are you sure you want to continue?
migration.confirmDescriptionWithBranches: This will modify the {{targetBranchName}} schema in {{projectName}} using the schema from {{sourceBranchName}}. Are you sure you want to continue?
migration.backupWarning: Make sure you have database backups enabled otherwise you cannot undo this.

preview.title: Review migration SQL
preview.subtitle: These statements will run against {{targetBranchName}}.
preview.subtitleNoTarget: These statements will run against your production database.
preview.noChanges: Your development and production databases are already in sync. Nothing to migrate.
preview.reviewAdvisory: Statements likely to cause data loss are marked with a warning icon and highlighted in red. Detection is approximate — review every statement before continuing.

destructive.title: Destructive changes detected
destructive.subtitle_one: {{count}} statement will permanently remove or alter existing data.
destructive.subtitle_other: {{count}} statements will permanently remove or alter existing data.

reasons.alter_column_type: A column's type will be changed (data conversion).
reasons.schema_hazard: This statement includes a database hazard such as a permission, lock, dependency, or data-safety risk.

migration.confirmDestructiveWarning: This migration includes destructive changes that may result in data loss.

mutualExclusion.supabaseUnavailable: Supabase is unavailable because a Neon database is already connected. Only one database integration can be active at a time.
mutualExclusion.neonUnavailable: Neon is unavailable because a Supabase project is already connected. Only one database integration can be active at a time.
mutualExclusion.chooseOne: Choose one database integration. Connecting either Supabase or Neon will lock out the other for this app.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Connect plugins (MCP servers) to give the AI new tools.
plugins.commandRequired: Command is required for stdio MCP servers.
plugins.urlRequired: URL is required for HTTP MCP servers.
plugins.urlProtocol: URL must use http:// or https://
plugins.connectDescription: Connect an MCP server to give the AI new tools.
plugins.name: Name
plugins.oauthClientIdDescription: If the MCP server's setup requires you to register an app, paste the Client ID of your app here. Otherwise leave this blank.
plugins.oauthClientSecretDescription: Include this only if the MCP server gave you a secret alongside the Client ID.
plugins.oauthScopeDescription: Permissions to request, space-separated. Leave this blank to use the server's default.
plugins.redirectUriDescription: If you include a Client ID, register {{uri}} as a redirect URI for your MCP server. Your MCP server most likely provides a dashboard where you can do this.
plugins.toolsWaitingForConnection: Tools will be listed once the server is connected.
plugins.oauthConnectionFailed: OAuth connection failed. This server doesn't support OAuth.
plugins.serverRequiresAuth: This server requires authentication. Enable OAuth and try again.
plugins.serverConnectionFailed: Server connection failed. This server requires authentication. Try enabling OAuth.
plugins.oauthStorageTitle: OAuth tokens and client secrets stored without OS encryption
plugins.oauthStorageDescription: Your OS keyring is unavailable (on Linux this usually means {{libsecret}}/{{gnomeKeyring}} is not installed), so OAuth tokens and pre-registered client secrets for HTTP MCP servers are written to the local database without encryption. Any process with read access to the Dyad data directory can decode them. Client secrets are especially sensitive because they don't expire. Install a keyring service and reconnect (and re-enter any pre-registered client secret) to upgrade.

templates.title: Pick your default template

proMode.searchReplaceTooltip: Find and replaces specific text blocks
proMode.smartContextTooltip: Selects the most relevant files as context to save credits working on large codebases.

proMode.deepTooltip: Experimental: Keeps full conversation history for maximum context and cache-optimized to control costs

appDetails.copyWithHistoryDescription: Copies the entire app, including the Git version history.
appDetails.copyWithoutHistoryDescription: Useful if the current app has a Git-related issue.
appDetails.changeLocationDescription: Select a folder where this app will be stored. The app folder name will remain the same.
appDetails.deleteDescription: This action is irreversible. All app files and chat history will be permanently deleted.
